prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: Hurt — Johnny Cash (2002). It moves me because Cash’s weathered voice and the spare, unornamented arrangement recast a song about despair into a stark reckoning with time, regret, and dignity. The spaces between phrases feel like measured breaths, and when he admits “I will let you down, I will make you hurt,” it lands as plainspoken truth rather than drama. Covering Trent Reznor’s original, Cash transforms youthful anguish into an elder’s inventory of losses, so by the final refrain it sounds like both confession and benediction.
